mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically lifting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals peak economic activity and impending earnings revisions downward, causing equity prices to discount future cash flows at higher risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the rising unemployment signal, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous stagnation in real GDP growth and elevated consumer price inflation compresses corporate profit margins through higher input costs and weaker volume demand, forcing equity multiples to contract and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60-day returns when GDP growth exceeds 2.5% annually, or the interaction term shows no statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in industrial production reflects recovering capacity utilization and stronger corporate throughput, which directly boosts near-term revenue expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth rates show no correlation with subsequent 5-day SPX returns, or the signal reverses direction during periods of supply chain normalization.

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts coupled with robust nonfarm payroll growth indicate broad-based consumer spending resilience, which supports earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns when both indicators exceed their respective thresholds.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative or neutral 20-day returns in > 50% of observations, or the marginal effect of PAYEMS becomes insignificant when HOUST is held constant.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent consumer price inflation above target forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, which elevates the equity risk premium and compresses valuation multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL levels show no monotonic relationship with 60-day forward SPX returns, or high inflation periods consistently produce positive equity returns without corresponding earnings growth.

mechanism_narrative: Rising revolving consumer credit balances alongside a stable unemployment rate signal expanding household purchasing power and confidence, which fuels corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short-term horizon.
failure_condition: The joint behavior of RSAFS and UNRATE fails to predict positive 5-day returns, or rising credit balances without employment growth produces negative equity returns as predicted by the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP growth below the historical trend rate signals weakening aggregate demand, which triggers downward earnings revisions and forces equity valuations to reset, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive or neutral when GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below random chance over a 3-year walk-forward test.
